chunks.text: [MassDevice](https://www.massdevice.com/) The Medical Device Business Journal — Medical Device News & Articles | MassDevice # Latest News on MassDevice ## [Weekly Wireless Roundup: NEJM iPhone app tests docs’ diagnosis skills](https://www.massdevice.com/weekly-wireless-roundup-nejm-iphone-app-tests-docs-diagnosis-skills/) December 29, 2010 By [MassDevice Contributors Network](https://www.massdevice.com/author/massdevice/) [Leave a Comment](https://www.massdevice.com/weekly-wireless-roundup-nejm-iphone-app-tests-docs-diagnosis-skills/#respond) **[NEJM iPhone app tests docs’ diagnosis skills](http://www.medgadget.com/archives/2010/12/nejm_image_challenge_now_available_on_ios.html):** *The New England Journal of Medicine* released its popular [Image Challenge](http://itunes.apple.com/us/app/nejm-image-challenge/id403219522?mt=8 "iTunes store"), a quiz testing healthcare providers’ ability to diagnose conditions based on photos, for Apple’s (NSDQ:[AAPL](http://www.google.com/finance?q=aapl "AAPL ticker")) iPhone/iPad platform. The app’s features include: Filed Under: [Digital Health](https://www.massdevice.com/category/mhealth/), [News Well](https://www.massdevice.com/category/news_well/), [Software / IT](https://www.massdevice.com/category/hightechnology/software_it/) Tagged With: [Alphabet (Google)](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/alphabet-google/), [Apple](https://www.massdevice.com/tag/apple/) ## [MassDevice New Year’s Special | Brent Hudson](https://www.massdevice.com/massdevice-new-years-special-brent-hudson/) December 27, 2010 By [MassDevice staff](https://www.massdevice.com/author/massdevice_staff/) [Leave a Comment](https://www.massdevice.com/massdevice-new-years-special-brent-hudson/#respond) ![Brent Hudson](https://www.massdevice.com/sites/default/files/story_art/2010/Hudson_Brent_200x300.jpg "Brent Hudson") Brent Hudson CEO, Sagentia Advisors Brent Hudson is the CEO of technology and product development consulting firm Sagentia and his company is bearing witness to both an improving economy for entrepreneurs and a regulatory system that is becoming harder to grapple with for small companies. Cambridge, U.K.-based Sagentia [opened up an office in tech-heavy Cambridge, Mass.](https://www.massdevice.com/node/7932/ "MassDevice.com news") in November; Hudson offered his predictions for an industry of whose growth he hopes to take advantage.
